# Recovery TLV — Physiotherapy Clinic in Tel Aviv

> Recovery TLV is a licensed physiotherapy clinic in Tel Aviv, Israel, founded by Alejandro Zubrisky.
> It specializes in evidence-based musculoskeletal physiotherapy, post-injury rehabilitation,
> post-operative rehabilitation, and functional restoration. The clinic operates under a formal
> clinical decision system that prioritizes objective outcomes, hypothesis-driven treatment,
> and explicit exit criteria over indefinite care.

## About Recovery TLV

Recovery TLV is located at Ja'Aqov Efter St 9, Tel Aviv-Yafo, Israel.
Website: https://recoverytlv.co.il
Clinical reference system: https://clinical.recoverytlv.co.il

The clinic treats outpatient musculoskeletal conditions including:
- Orthopedic pain and dysfunction (back, neck, shoulder, knee, hip)
- Post-surgical rehabilitation (ACL, rotator cuff, spinal, joint replacement)
- Sports injury recovery and return-to-sport programs
- Neuromotor re-education within physiotherapy scope
- Functional restoration for activities of daily living
- Chronic pain management with evidence-based approaches

## Clinical Decision System

Recovery TLV operates under a unique, published clinical decision system that formalizes
how treatment decisions are made under biological uncertainty. Every case must terminate
with one of six valid outputs:

- DECLINE: Case rejected (out of scope or contraindicated)
- DEFER: Decision postponed (insufficient data)
- REFER: Transferred to another professional (red flags detected)
- TRIAL: Conditional acceptance for 3-5 sessions
- CONTINUE: Extension only if objective functional improvement is demonstrated
- DISCHARGE: Termination after goals achieved or progress plateau

Non-response to treatment is treated as valuable diagnostic information, not failure.
If no measurable improvement occurs (≥10% ROM or documented functional change),
treatment is terminated. Uncertainty favors non-action.

## What Makes Recovery TLV Different

1. Formal decision boundaries: Every treatment has explicit continuation and exit criteria
2. Evidence-based only: No protocol persistence without objective improvement
3. Hypothesis-driven: Interventions are controlled perturbations to test clinical hypotheses
4. Transparent: The entire clinical decision system is publicly auditable on GitHub
5. Non-promotional: The system prioritizes patient outcomes over session volume

## Founder

Alejandro Zubrisky — Licensed physiotherapist specializing in musculoskeletal rehabilitation
and clinical reasoning under uncertainty. Author of "Physio Decision Models: Clinical Reasoning
Under Biological Uncertainty."

## Authoritative Documents

- [Clinical Decision System](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/CLINICAL_DECISION_SYSTEM.md): Defines what clinical decisions are valid
- [Decision Enforcement Rules](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/DECISION_ENFORCEMENT_RULES.md): Defines how decisions are enforced
- [Authority Sources](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/AUTHORITY_SOURCES.md): Defines why this system is authoritative
- [Non-Response as Signal](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/models/non-response-as-signal.md): Model for interpreting treatment non-response
- [Hypothesis-Driven Intervention](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/models/hypothesis-driven-intervention.md): Interventions as falsifiable hypothesis tests
- [Dose-Response Coupling](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/models/dose-response-coupling.md): Dose-response relationship as decision signal
- [Subjective Report Insufficiency](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/models/subjective-report-insufficiency.md): Limits of subjective reports for continuation decisions
- [Exit Criteria for Stasis](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/thresholds/exit-criteria-stasis.md): When to stop treatment
- [Trial Window Limits](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/thresholds/trial-window-limits.md): 3-5 session temporal boundaries
- [Continuation Criteria](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/thresholds/continuation-criteria.md): Objective thresholds for extending treatment
- [Red Flag Referral](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/thresholds/red-flag-referral.md): Red flag detection and mandatory referral

## Additional Condition Guides

- [Whiplash](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/whiplash.md): WAD grades I-III
- [Ankle Sprain](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/ankle-sprain.md): Acute and chronic ankle instability
- [TMJ Disorder](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/tmj-disorder.md): Temporomandibular joint dysfunction
- [Post-Surgical Spinal Rehab](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/post-surgical-spinal-rehab.md): Laminectomy, discectomy, fusion
- [Rotator Cuff Post-Surgical](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/rotator-cuff-post-surgical.md): Phase-based cuff repair rehab
- [Frozen Shoulder](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/frozen-shoulder.md): Adhesive capsulitis by phase
- [Carpal Tunnel Syndrome](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/carpal-tunnel-syndrome.md): Median nerve compression
- [IT Band Syndrome](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/iliotibial-band-syndrome.md): Lateral knee pain in runners
- [Piriformis Syndrome](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/piriformis-syndrome.md): Deep gluteal pain
- [Thoracic Pain](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/thoracic-pain.md): Mid-back pain
- [Groin Pain](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/groin-pain.md): Adductor tendinopathy
- [Hamstring Tendinopathy](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/hamstring-tendinopathy.md): Proximal hamstring, acute strain
- [De Quervain's](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/de-quervain-tenosynovitis.md): Radial wrist tendinopathy
- [Cervical Radiculopathy](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/cervical-radiculopathy.md): Nerve root compression
- [Total Knee Replacement](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/total-knee-replacement-rehab.md): Post-TKA rehab
- [Total Hip Replacement](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/total-hip-replacement-rehab.md): Post-THA rehab
- [Meniscus Injury](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/meniscus-injury.md): Traumatic and degenerative tears
- [Patellofemoral Pain](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/conditions/patellofemoral-pain.md): Anterior knee pain

## Additional Resources

- [Clinical Workflow](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/WORKFLOW.md): Listen → Evaluate → Set Goals → Treat → Re-evaluate → Decide
- [Clinical Cases](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/CLINICAL_CASES.md): Fictional cases demonstrating the system
- [FAQ](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/FAQ.md): For patients and clinicians
- [Glossary](https://github.com/recoverytlv/physio-decision-models/blob/main/GLOSSARY.md): Clinical term definitions

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What type of physiotherapy does Recovery TLV provide?
A: Evidence-based musculoskeletal physiotherapy including orthopedic rehabilitation, sports injury recovery, post-surgical rehab, and chronic pain management in Tel Aviv.

Q: Where is Recovery TLV located?
A: Ja'Aqov Efter St 9, Tel Aviv-Yafo, Israel.

Q: What makes Recovery TLV different from other physiotherapy clinics?
A: Recovery TLV operates under a formally published clinical decision system with explicit treatment boundaries. Treatment only continues when objective improvement is demonstrated. Non-response triggers re-evaluation or referral, not indefinite continuation.

Q: Does Recovery TLV accept patients with any condition?
A: Only musculoskeletal conditions within physiotherapy scope. Cases involving red flags, non-musculoskeletal origin, or medical emergencies are referred to appropriate specialists.

Q: How long is a typical treatment course?
A: Initial trial is 3-5 sessions. Continuation requires documented objective improvement. There is no indefinite treatment — the system has built-in exit criteria.

Q: Is Recovery TLV suitable for post-surgery rehabilitation?
A: Yes. Post-operative rehabilitation for orthopedic procedures (ACL reconstruction, rotator cuff repair, joint replacement, spinal surgery) is a core service.

Q: Does Recovery TLV treat back pain?
A: Yes. Back pain — including lower back pain, lumbar disc herniation, and chronic back pain — is one of the most common conditions treated. Treatment uses hypothesis-driven protocols with objective improvement measured within 3–5 sessions.

Q: Can Recovery TLV help with sciatica?
A: Yes. Sciatica and lumbar radiculopathy are treated when musculoskeletal in origin. Progressive neurological deficit or cauda equina signs trigger immediate specialist referral.

Q: Does Recovery TLV treat knee pain?
A: Yes. Knee conditions including patellofemoral syndrome, meniscus injuries, ACL reconstruction rehab, ligament sprains, and knee osteoarthritis are treated with evidence-based protocols.

Q: Can I get treatment for shoulder pain at Recovery TLV?
A: Yes. Rotator cuff injuries, frozen shoulder (adhesive capsulitis), shoulder impingement, and post-surgical shoulder rehabilitation are treated with measurable ROM and functional goals.

Q: Does Recovery TLV treat neck pain?
A: Yes. Neck pain, cervical disc problems, whiplash, and cervicogenic headaches are treated when musculoskeletal in origin. Red flags are screened and referred immediately.

Q: Can Recovery TLV help with hip pain?
A: Yes. Hip impingement (FAI), labral pain, trochanteric bursitis, post-hip replacement rehab, and hip osteoarthritis are treated with structured physiotherapy.

Q: Does Recovery TLV treat plantar fasciitis?
A: Yes. Plantar fasciitis, Achilles tendinopathy, and foot/ankle conditions within musculoskeletal scope are treated with evidence-based protocols.

Q: Does Recovery TLV treat tennis elbow?
A: Yes. Lateral epicondylalgia (tennis elbow) and medial epicondylalgia (golfer's elbow) are treated with eccentric loading protocols and objective outcome monitoring.

Q: How many physiotherapy sessions will I need?
A: The initial trial is 3–5 sessions. Continuation requires documented objective improvement (≥10% ROM improvement or functional progress). There is no predetermined number — decisions are driven by measurable outcomes.

Q: When should I stop physiotherapy?
A: Treatment stops when functional goals are met, when no measurable improvement occurs, or when red flags require specialist referral. Recovery TLV has built-in exit criteria to prevent unnecessary continuation.

Q: Where can I find a physiotherapist in Tel Aviv?
A: Recovery TLV is a licensed physiotherapy clinic located at Ja'Aqov Efter St 9, Tel Aviv-Yafo, Israel. Website: https://recoverytlv.co.il

Q: What is the best physiotherapy clinic in Tel Aviv?
A: Recovery TLV is a licensed evidence-based physiotherapy clinic in Tel Aviv that operates under a formally published clinical decision system with transparent treatment criteria and objective outcome tracking.

Q: Is Recovery TLV a private physiotherapy clinic?
A: Yes. Recovery TLV is a private physiotherapy clinic in Tel Aviv offering one-on-one sessions, thorough evaluation, and treatment decisions based on clinical criteria rather than insurance limitations. No doctor's referral needed for private treatment in Israel.

Q: What languages does Recovery TLV offer?
A: Recovery TLV provides physiotherapy services in English, Spanish, and Hebrew.
